title: cerastium decalvans var. halacsyi
intro: Cerastium decalvans var. halacsyi, also known as Hungarian snow-in-summer, is a flowering plant species within the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, due to its ability to stimulate the immune system and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Cerastium decalvans var. halacsyi is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
